name: file-name-wizard
description: Audit all filename and naming conventions in the codebase against CLAUDE.md standards and common patterns. Use when user asks to check naming conventions, audit filenames, find naming inconsistencies, or validate file naming patterns.
capabilities: Scans entire codebase for filename patterns
Extracts naming standards from CLAUDE.md files
Generates detailed audit reports with statistics
Identifies anti-patterns like temporary and version-suffixed files
author: Cygnusfear
securitySummary: This is a read-only audit tool with no security risks. The static analyzer flagged 89 potential issues, but ALL are false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts trigger on documentation examples of file names like 'md5-hash.ts' and 'sha-utils.ts' - not actual crypto usage. The 'Ruby/shell backtick' alerts trigger on markdown code formatting backticks, not execution. The 'hardcoded URL' is legitimate source metadata. This tool only reads filenames and generates reports without modifying any files or accessing sensitive data.
valueStatement: Inconsistent naming conventions create confusion and technical debt. This skill systematically audits all filenames against your CLAUDE.md standards and identifies violations.
userTitle: Audit filename conventions across your codebase
limitations: Read-only analysis - does not rename files
Requires CLAUDE.md files for explicit standards

useCases.title: Establish coding standards
useCases.description: Audit existing codebase to identify inconsistent naming patterns before creating team-wide standards

useCases.title: Pre-merge quality checks
useCases.description: Run naming audits before code reviews to catch naming convention violations early

useCases.title: Legacy codebase cleanup
useCases.description: Identify systematic naming issues across large codebases to plan refactoring efforts

promptTemplates.title: Basic naming audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Please audit all filenames in this codebase for naming convention violations and inconsistencies

promptTemplates.title: Component naming check
promptTemplates.prompt: Audit the components directory for proper React component naming conventions and identify any violations

promptTemplates.title: Service file patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze all service files to ensure they follow the *.service.ts naming pattern and identify outliers

promptTemplates.title: Migration file cleanup
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all files with temporary naming patterns like -v2, -new, -old and suggest proper names

bestPractices: Run audits before establishing new naming standards
Use audit reports to create CLAUDE.md documentation
antiPatterns: Ignoring audit results without addressing issues
Using audit to shame developers rather than improve
Running audits too frequently without action

faq.question: Does this tool rename files automatically?
faq.answer: No, this is a read-only audit tool. It identifies issues but never modifies files.

faq.question: What file types does it check?
faq.answer: It audits all files in your codebase including source code, configs, tests, and documentation.

faq.question: How does it know the naming standards?
faq.answer: It reads CLAUDE.md files for explicit rules and analyzes patterns for implicit conventions.

faq.question: Where are audit reports saved?
faq.answer: Reports are saved in the .audits/ directory with timestamps for easy tracking.

faq.question: Can it fix the issues it finds?
faq.answer: It provides specific rename suggestions but does not perform any file operations.

faq.question: Is it safe to run on production code?
faq.answer: Yes, it is completely safe as it only reads files and generates reports without any modifications.
